render_evidence.basis: hand-stamp: live render + structural parity + human review; the placeholder-only source migration preserves byte-identical post-bind XML versus the stamped literal-token form, proven by templatePlaceholderPilots.test.ts
render_evidence.lane: generic-template live-verify 2026-07-04 (original four: kpi / part-to-whole / ranking / time-series)
render_evidence.critical_pass: not recorded (legacy hand-stamp; earned by human review, pass-list ratio not retained)
render_evidence.high_pass: not recorded (legacy hand-stamp; earned by human review, pass-list ratio not retained)
render_evidence.pixel_oracle: none (generic template has no golden anchor; an anchor-only comparison is circular and is not credited)

description: Horizontal bars, one per category; bar length = measure; sorted descending by the measure.
avoid_when: The 'top' here is positional layering — marks drawn on top of a reference line or another chart layer — not a top-N ranking; a positional or annotation placement is not a sorted bar chart.

slots.purpose: Categorical dimension that defines one ranked horizontal bar per member.
Continuous measure that controls bar length and descending rank order.
Optional categorical dimension that partitions the ranking into row-oriented small multiples.
slots.notes: OPTIONAL small-multiples facet (W23-SM1): a spare categorical dimension the binder places on rows AHEAD of the ranked category pill, trellising the ranking into stacked panes (one ranked bar set per member). Optional → EXCLUDED from the required-bindable fixture_bind set (only {region, sales} count), so fast_path_eligible does not move. Binds ONLY when the ask names/implies a by-<dim> facet (small multiples / trellis / per <dim> / for each) AND a spare categorical remains after the required slots — it never steals the ranked (region) dim, and a bare 'by <dim>' never facets (fail-closed). XML declares {{field_base_3}} as a datasource-dependency column the field-swap injector renames to the bound dim; on-shelf pill placement for a bound facet is applied downstream (the default sorted-bar render is byte-unchanged). Modeled on box-plot-chart's required:false facet.

hazards.detail: computed-sort binds the dimension instance to the measure instance; both slots must bind or the sort dangles (both are required, so this is satisfied by coverage).
